title: Replace the truncator with a mode-aware PolicyEngine gating every outbound LLM and embedding call
description: NexusMode-driven gate: LOCAL blocks all external incl. embeddings (fail-closed); HYBRID forces local embeddings + redacts/caps + inspects payload and blocks on secret/PII; CLOUD explicit. Route index_documents embed step through it. Real PII/secret regexes + payload inspector. Phase 1-2.
close_reason: Replaced the truncator PolicyRedactor with PolicyEngine: NexusMode-driven single gate on ALL outbound (guard_llm + guard_embedding). LOCAL blocks any external call incl. embeddings (fail-closed); HYBRID forces local embeddings + refuses secret-bearing payloads; CLOUD explicit but still refuses secrets. Real PII redaction (email/ssn/phone/cc) + high-confidence secret scan (aws/openai/anthropic/google/github/slack/private-key) returning pattern NAMES not values. prepare_context redacts PII + caps + attributes; payload inspector -> PolicyDecision(provider,model,chars,tokens,chunk ids,hashes,redactions,secret hits,decision) with as_receipt(); PolicyViolation on block. Added PrivacyReceipt model on QueryResponse. 22 policy unit tests green.

title: PR2: Cover the moat invariants — all 8 secret + 4 PII patterns, provider adapters, ABC contract test

notes: PR #13 opened: moat coverage (all 8 secret + 4 PII patterns, provider adapters SDK-mocked, ABC contract, cite-success, single-gate AST guard). Coverage 69->78%, providers 20-23->58-82%, CRAP truthful (43->4 nexus blockers), floor 65->75. 182 tests green. Awaiting CI+Gemini.
close_reason: PR #13 merged (squash). All 8 secret + 4 PII patterns parametrized (positive+near-miss), provider adapters behaviorally tested (SDK-mocked, 39 tests), ABC locality+health-check contract, cite-success unit test, single-gate AST guard. Coverage 69->78%, providers 20-23->58-82%, CRAP truthful via coverage.json (43->4 nexus blockers), floor 65->75. 182 tests green. 2 Gemini comments (relative-path guard bug + robust mock) fixed.

title: Add tests proving the embed path, LOCAL zero-egress, cloud secret-block, and provider fallback
description: New 03-Tests: OpenAI/Vertex embed no AttributeError; LOCAL makes zero network calls (network-blocked stub) incl embeddings; sentinel secret in a chunk blocked before any LLM or embedding cloud call; fallback picks next provider. Phase 1-2.
close_reason: Added 03-Tests/test_privacy_gate.py (8 E2E tests with in-process fakes): OpenAI embed path indexes via real chromadb with no AttributeError; LOCAL mode makes zero network calls (socket-blocked) + blocks cloud providers; sentinel AWS-key secret blocked before the cloud LLM call AND before the cloud embedding call (assert generate/embed never invoked); provider fallback skips erroring + unavailable preferred. Rewrote test_policy.py -> 22 PolicyEngine tests (redaction, secret scan, mode matrix, receipt). Repaired latent router tests (patch Config, not os.environ, which is read at import). Full unit gate: 62 passed, 4 skipped, exit 0; ruff clean.

title: Fix the embedding abstraction so OpenAI and Vertex embeddings work through the provider ABC
description: Stop calling private _get_embeddings; add a LangChain adapter over embed_documents/embed_query so Chroma consumes the ABC uniformly. Fixes the AttributeError crash on non-Ollama embed. Phase 1-2.
close_reason: Fixed the embedding abstraction: added _ABCEmbeddingAdapter (LangChain Embeddings over the provider ABC) so Chroma consumes embed_documents/embed_query uniformly — OpenAI/Vertex embeddings no longer crash on the Ollama-only private _get_embeddings(). Also fixed the langchain.text_splitter->langchain_text_splitters import (dep drift, langchain 1.3). Routed BOTH the index embed step and the query question-embed through policy.guard_embedding (fail-closed), and the LLM call through policy.guard_llm; prepare_context for redaction/capping; explicit untrusted-context boundary + insufficient-evidence refusal in the prompt; builds a PrivacyReceipt per query; retriever.invoke (langchain 1.x). Verified: OpenAI embed path indexes via real chromadb with no AttributeError.

title: Refactor NEXUS into a local-first BYOK document-intelligence platform
description:
Turn the naive local-RAG app (product "NEXUS", repo iam-local-rag) into a local-first / hybrid / cloud-BYOK document-intelligence platform whose moat is verifiable citations, privacy receipts, provider portability, and a policy-bounded outbound path.

This epic is the "revive" answer to the dormant decision epic (local-3py "Revive or archive NEXUS").

Acceptance invariants (enforced in code + tests):
1. Every cloud/model call (LLM AND embeddings) passes through one policy gate. No second path.
2. LOCAL mode makes zero external calls (incl. embeddings), fail-closed.
3. Every answer is cited or explicitly says evidence is insufficient.
4. Every query can emit a privacy receipt (provider/model, chars/tokens out, chunk ids+hashes, redactions, policy pass/fail, local-vs-cloud).
5. Retrieved document text is untrusted data, never instructions.
6. No secret in code; no key in logs; privacy never silently degraded.
7. Typed, tested, small coherent commits.

SDK strategy: thin per-provider adapters behind our own LLMProvider/EmbeddingProvider ABCs; NO agent-SDK in core. Knowledge-bank reuse: optional qmd hybrid+rerank backend, Chroma default; tamper-evident ledger borrows ICO's hash-chained govern/audit. Ships standalone (zero-brain works).

This pass = Phase 0 (audit + hygiene) + Phase 1-2 (dangerous-bug fixes + provider correctness). Phases 3-7 are roadmap children.

HARD CONSTRAINT: Docs stay local; cloud receives only retrieved snippets+hashes. Acceptance: All phases complete, AAR written, tests passing.

close_reason: PR #14 merged (squash). Lazy get_ledger DI + injectable RAGPipeline ledger= (killed import- AND construction-time ./nexus_ledger.db leak, verified zero strays); blocking test_api_mocked.py + shared conftest mocked_api fixture; test_api_auth rewired; CLI cmd_ask/cmd_audit tests (cli 72->90%); 2 integration tests fixed to assert real redaction/truncation (verified vs local Ollama); refusal-receipt assertion; tmp_path hygiene; floor 75->78; mutation scope settled to pure-logic. 202 tests, 79.67% cov. 1 lint fix + 2 Gemini isolation comments addressed.

title: Harden the integration CI job (Ollama model pull) and migrate off sunset langchain-community classes
description: PR #6 CI: required checks (lint, test 3.11/3.12, harness) all GREEN; the non-blocking (continue-on-error) integration job failed because ephemeral CI Ollama did not have the model ready for embed_documents (500 on /index+/query). The embed PATH is fine (reaches the Ollama API). Follow-ups: (1) make the integration job robust — ensure ollama pull completes / use a smaller embedding model / longer readiness wait, or mock. (2) Migrate OllamaEmbeddings + Ollama from the sunset langchain_community to langchain_ollama (deprecation warning; still works). Also revisit langchain_community.vectorstores.Chroma -> langchain_chroma. Non-blocking; unit gate (65 tests) proves the policy/adapter/provider logic.

title: Build the trust moat: hash-chained ledger, per-query privacy receipt, injection hardening, groundedness verifier, and API auth
description: P4: tamper-evident hash-chained ledger (prev_hash/row_hash, DELETE exception-gated, borrow ICO govern/audit-chain); privacy receipt per query; prompt-injection hardening + nexus/evals injection corpus; groundedness/citation-coverage verifier before final answer; API auth + real workspace/tenant boundary. Roadmap.
notes: Adversarial review of P3 confirmed the pre-existing wildcard CORS (allow_origins=['*'] + allow_credentials) on the unauthenticated /query + /index endpoints (server.py) is a real drive-by/CSRF vector. Fold into this P4 bead (API auth + tenant boundary): restrict CORS to an allowlist + add auth on mutating endpoints. The qmd arg-injection that made it remotely exploitable is already fixed in P3.

title: Modularize retrieval and ingestion into stage interfaces and add hybrid plus rerank with a qmd backend and Chroma fallback
description: P3: extract DocumentLoader/Chunker/Embedder/VectorStore/Retriever/Reranker/ContextPacker/AnswerGenerator/CitationVerifier/RunLedger under nexus/retrieval + nexus/ingestion; dense+BM25+RRF, MMR, multi-query, rerank; default qmd backend (reuse), Chroma fallback; real citations; insufficient-evidence refusal; answer modes; multi-format ingestion + incremental. Roadmap.

Repointed the 5 legacy-coupled tests: test_imports.py + test_streamlit_smoke.py now test the nexus package + parse app_nexus.py (no legacy app.py/app_optimized dependency) — they run (not skip) and pass. Unit gate 65 passed.

title: Revive or archive NEXUS local-rag-agent: settle the dormant backlog and decide the project's future
description: Backlog Zero campaign (Wave 0, 2026-07-02) settled the dormant backlog: all 4 open beads (the NEXUS Hybrid Cloud Upgrade epic and its Phase 2/3/4 tasks) were done-but-open drift — the same work was re-tracked and closed under the later 'NEXUS Phases 2-4' epic (10 children, all closed) and shipped as v1.1.0 'Hybrid Cloud Upgrade' (tag + release report 000-docs/501-REPT-nexus-release-v1.1.0.md; providers/router/policy/ledger/API in nexus/, tests in 03-Tests/, CI in .github/workflows/). Revival needs: (1) decide whether NEXUS remains a live product line or gets archived (last substantive commit was the v1.1.0 era; only bd-init since); (2) if reviving, refresh the LangChain/ChromaDB/Ollama dependency pins and re-verify CI; (3) settle the stray feat/nexus-rebrand and chore/portfolio-upgrade remote branches.
notes:
GitHub: jeremylongshore/iam-local-rag#5 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/iam-local-rag/issues/5. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note local-3py` and `bd-sync close local-3py` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
